issues.description: Wire 'mem_req_active' is assigned but never used in the design. It duplicates the functionality of the condition check already done inline in the always @(*) block.
issues.fix_suggestion: Remove the unused wire declaration on line 14, or use it in the dmem_req assignment to replace the inline condition.

issues.description: Redundant conditionals for funct3=3'b000 (LB/SB): The read and write branches compute identical dmem_be values. Same issue exists for funct3=3'b001 (LH/SH) on lines 35-40.
issues.fix_suggestion: Simplify to: 'if (mem_read | mem_write) begin dmem_be = ...; end else begin dmem_be = 4'b0000; end' for these cases, since both branches produce the same result.

issues.description: Ternary operator in dmem_be assignment lacks explicit parentheses around condition: '(mem_read | mem_write) ? 4'b1111 : 4'b0000'. While operator precedence makes this functionally correct, explicit grouping improves readability and avoids potential confusion.

summary: The mem_stage module is functionally correct and implements the required memory access stage functionality. All byte enable generation (LB/LBU/LH/LHU/LW/SB/SH/SW), data alignment, sign/zero extension, and pipeline register passing are properly implemented. No critical or high-severity issues found. The code uses correct blocking assignments in combinational always @(*) blocks and non-blocking assignments in the sequential always_ff block. Reset handling is correct with async reset. Case statements have proper defaults preventing latch inference. Minor code quality issues (unused wire, redundant conditionals) do not affect functionality.
